title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
summary: Garrett Galow presented Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-engineers query Snowflake, Linear, and Notion using natural language. The tool uses LLMs to generate reusable JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, avoiding repetitive SQL requests. Three reliability techniques enable it for production use: preflight sequencing for context injection, explicit model layering to avoid hallucination, and query validation before widget hardcoding.
writeup: Garrett Galow of WorkOS describes Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. Rather than answering each query with a fresh LLM call, the system generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call the data sources directly. Subsequent runs of those widgets are deterministic and cheap, since the generated code, not the model, executes the query. The talk touches on schema injection, context management, and query validation as part of making this reliable.
